prompt: The bubble sort does not complete sorting. What is the bug?
choices: The outer loop should count down instead of up
The inner loop bound a.length-1 should be a.length-i-1; current code still works but does redundant comparisons on already-sorted tail
int t must be declared outside the loop
The swap condition should use >= instead of >
explanation: The code is functionally correct but not optimized. After each outer pass, the largest unsorted element bubbles to position a.length-i-1. Using a.length-i-1 as the inner bound avoids re-examining already-placed elements. The question tests whether students spot inefficiency vs. actual bugs.
